name: News Worker Example
description: A complete News-like worker anatomy example with configuration, health, job ownership, dashboard output, and future entrypoints.

ownedSettings.label: News search configuration
ownedSettings.description: Schema-driven query and limit settings shown centrally in the Config tab.

ownedSettings.label: Google Search credentials
ownedSettings.description: Placeholder-owned environment values for GOOGLE_API_KEY and GOOGLE_SEARCH_ENGINE_ID; secrets are never stored in worker.json.

ownedSettings.label: News digest schedule
ownedSettings.description: Enable flag, cron expression, model override, and approval settings for a News-like digest job.

ownedSettings.label: News digest output
ownedSettings.description: Fetched, filtered, queued, and rejected news items visualized in a worker-owned dashboard tab.

settings.label: Search configuration
settings.description: Worker-provided form metadata and defaults for queries, date restriction, and digest limits.

fields.label: Search queries
fields.helpText: One query per line. The installer exposes these defaults from the manifest.

fields.label: Date restrict
fields.helpText: Google Custom Search date restriction, such as d1, d7, or m1.

fields.label: Max results per query
fields.helpText: Upper bound for search results fetched for each query.

fields.label: Send selected items to queue
fields.helpText: Example boolean setting owned by the worker.

settings.label: Google Search credentials
settings.description: Placeholder credential surface for API key and search engine ID. Store real values in local environment, not in the manifest.

fields.label: Google API key
fields.helpText: Placeholder only. Real secrets belong in local environment storage.

fields.label: Search engine ID
fields.helpText: Stored locally as GOOGLE_SEARCH_ENGINE_ID by a real worker endpoint.

routes.label: Digest dashboard
routes.description: Worker-owned output view for run metrics and queued news items, shown as a dedicated tab named after the worker.
